lede: A look at November 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Liz (South Korean singer) and Larry Sanders (basketball), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2012 Tel Aviv bus bombing
events.desc: At least 28 are wounded after a bomb is thrown onto a bus in Tel Aviv.

events.title: 2009 Heilongjiang mine explosion
events.desc: A mine explosion in Heilongjiang, China kills 108.

events.title: MGM Grand fire
events.desc: A deadly fire breaks out at the MGM Grand Hotel in Paradise, Nevada (now Bally's Las Vegas). Eighty-five people are killed and more than 650 are injured in the worst disaster in Nevada history.

events.title: Birmingham pub bombings
events.desc: The Birmingham pub bombings kill 21 people. The Birmingham Six are sentenced to life in prison for the crime but are later exonerated.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: American General William Westmoreland tells news reporters: "I am absolutely certain that whereas in 1965 the enemy was winning, today he is certainly losing."

events.title: Verrazzano–Narrows Bridge
events.desc: The Verrazzano–Narrows Bridge opens to traffic. At the time it is the world's longest bridge span.

events.title: La Ronde (restaurant)
events.desc: "La Ronde" opens in Honolulu, the first revolving restaurant in the United States.

events.title: Rebecca Latimer Felton
events.desc: Rebecca Latimer Felton of Georgia takes the oath of office, becoming the first female United States Senator.

events.title: Irish War of Independence
events.desc: Irish War of Independence: On "Bloody Sunday" in Dublin, the Irish Republican Army (IRA) assassinated a group of British Intelligence agents, and British forces killed 14 civilians at a Gaelic football match at Croke Park.

events.title: Thomas Edison
events.desc: Thomas Edison announces his invention of the phonograph, a machine that can record and play sound.

deaths.text: Alice Brock, American artist, author and restauranteur
Emily Squires, American director, producer, and screenwriter
Peter Grant (music manager), English actor and manager
Jean-Baptiste Henri Lacordaire, French priest and activist

observances.text: Christian feast days: Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary
Christian feast days: Rufus of Rome (no. 7 in list)
